title: Japan’s Nikkei 225 tops 65,000 for first time as oil falls on Hormuz reopening hopes
description: Japan’s Nikkei 225 breached 64,000 for the first time Monday as a sharp decline in oil prices lifted risk sentiment in holiday-thinned trading.

title: AI fever and off-exchange boom grip Japanese retail traders
description: Average daily trading volume on Tokyo Stock Exchange’s prime market almost doubles in 12 months

title: China Trading Curbs May Hit $32 Billion of Hong Kong Assets, Citic Says
description: China’s latest crackdown on cross-border stock trading aimed at tightening control over capital outflows may affect as much as HK$250 billion ($32 billion) of assets in Hong Kong, according to Citic Securities.

title: India Raises Diesel, Gasoline Prices for Fourth Time in May
description: India’s state-run fuel retailers raised gasoline and diesel prices for the fourth time in 10 days in a delayed response to the Iran war pushing up the cost of crude, a move that boosts inflationary risks.

title: AI Rally Drives Best Run in Global Momentum Stocks on Record
description: The AI boom is giving momentum investors their best return in decades, as the world’s hottest stocks power ahead despite worries over potentially slower growth due to the Iran war.

title: Sri Lanka Rupee to Recover From Plunge as Oil to Ease, BMI Says
description: The Sri Lankan rupee is set to recover from its slide by the end of this year as oil prices are set to decline and as the central bank raises interest rates, according to BMI.

title: Bank SMBC Indonesia Sells $1.1 Billion in Pension Loans to BTN
description: PT Bank SMBC Indonesia, a unit of Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp, is selling 19.9 trillion rupiah ($1.1 billion) worth of pension loans as it tilts away from its legacy business.

title: Chinese Mine Disaster Sparks Jump in Coal Prices and Stocks
description: Chinese coking coal futures jumped by the daily limit and mining stocks surged after a deadly accident in Shanxi province sparked fears of broader supply disruptions.

title: India Rupee Climbs on Oil Relief, Central Bank Valuation Comment
description: India’s rupee strengthened as oil prices dropped on hopes of a US-Iran deal and on comments by the central bank Governor that the currency may be undervalued.

title: FTSE Cut Hits Stock Tied to One of Indonesia’s Richest Families
description: Shares linked to one of Indonesia’s richest families dropped after FTSE Russell over the weekend removed the company from its global indexes due to concentrated shareholding.

title: India Rupee May Be Undervalued, RBI Governor Tells Mint
description: The Indian rupee may now be undervalued following its recent depreciation, Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra said in an interview with Mint.

title: India State Lenders Wilt As Iran War Lifts Yields to 2-Year High
description: A Nifty gauge of government-controlled banks has fallen 6% this month.

title: India Bond Investors Tap Soaring Swap Rates to Juice Returns
description: Multi-year high swap rates are driving some Indian debt fund managers into trades aimed at boosting fixed-income returns.

title: Chinese Firms Speed Up Plans to Build New Coal Power Plants: GEM
description: Chinese firms are accelerating the pace at which they propose new coal-fired power plants, even as the government moves to rein in growth after the rapid expansion in recent years.

title: How Saudi Arabia's spending spree reached the end of the line
description: MBS's Vison 2030 project seemed the stuff of science fiction. Now reality has bitten

title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
description: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

title: 'I live in survival mode': The rise of the multi-job workforce
description: More people are taking second jobs as rising costs and insecure work reshape how we earn a living.

title: Beer boom goes flat as breweries call last orders
description: The UK's brewery scene is shrinking as pubs close, costs rise and drinking habits change.

title: How Wine, Truffles and Honey Could Help Europe Fight Wildfires
description: Countries are learning that the finer things in life sometimes have a serendipitous side benefit.

title: Pope Leo Is Set to Release an Encyclical About A.I. Why Is That Important?
description: Leo XIV is to issue his thoughts about artificial intelligence in the modern world, using a centuries-old form of papal communication called an encyclical, the first since he became pope.

title: Rubio Rebukes Hezbollah Chief Over Call for Lebanese to ‘Take to the Streets’
description: The militant leader, in a speech, had criticized the Lebanese government for engaging in direct talks with Israel.

title: The Oscars Start at Cannes
description: Glamorous and a touch old-fashioned, the French film festival increasingly defines what Hollywood celebrates each year.

title: $140,000 E.V.s and Heritage Gold: The Rise of China’s Homegrown Luxury Market
description: As China’s economy slows, consumers are increasingly turning to domestic luxury products, challenging the dominance of long-established European brands.

title: Oil Prices Fall on News of Possible Iran Deal
description: S&P 500 futures were up slightly in what may be a sign that investors and analysts are waiting to see if a deal is finalized.

title: ‘One man, one vote’? Maybe Not in Trump’s America
description: A recent Supreme Court decision threatens a core democratic principle.

title: Africa Day 2026: Has the continent achieved true liberation?
description: Africa Day 2026 spotlights power, debt and digital control as Africans redefine sovereignty.

title: Mexico City paints record-breaking football mural ahead of World Cup
description: Mexico City unveiled a mural that earned a Guinness World Record ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

title: Russia invites media to view deadly strike on college in Luhansk
description: Russia provided a rare look at damage caused by a Ukrainian strike on a college in occupied Luhansk

title: Building collapse in Philippines leaves 3 dead, 17 missing
description: Rescuers search for survivors after thermal scans show signs of breathing and heartbeats beneath debris.

title: Oil prices fall amid mixed signals on US-Iran peace deal
description: Japan's stock market surges to record high on hopes of an end to US-Israel war on Iran.

title: Syria holds legislative elections in former Kurdish-controlled areas
description: Al Jazeera’s Heidi Pett reports on the Syrian legislative elections in Hasakah and the town of Kobane.

title: Property challenges put Chinese firms’ go-global plans at risk, JLL says
description: Chinese companies have found it difficult to secure offices, logistics facilities and retail space when expanding their footprints abroad, forcing some to put their global expansion plans on hold due to failed property strategies, according to a JLL report. The real estate services firm said 82 per cent of corporate respondents in a survey reported either paying more than expected to buy or rent properties, or wasting time in failed searches or prolonged furnishing, representing a stern...

title: China’s machinery giant XCMG eyes bigger scoop of global sales with green, autonomous tech
description: XCMG Group, mainland China’s largest construction machinery maker, aims to achieve annual overseas sales of 100 billion yuan (US$14.7 billion), luring more international clients by turning its excavators, cranes and specialised vehicles greener and more automated. Yang Dongsheng, chairman of the state-owned machinery giant, told reporters in a media briefing last week that the target would be achieved in line with the company’s ambition of building itself into a top-class machinery producer...

title: How Xiaomi’s push into AI, chips and EVs is future-proofing its hardware empire
description: Xiaomi is betting on artificial intelligence to future-proof its sprawling hardware empire, pouring massive resources into open-source models to ensure its next generation of smartphones and electric vehicles (EVs) does not get left behind in an AI-driven market. The Beijing-based firm’s latest AI model, MiMo-V2.5-Pro, introduced last month, was ranked by third-party benchmark platform Artificial Analysis as the world’s top open-source model for agentic capabilities – referring to an AI system’s...

title: Rich environments: Hong Kong banks pour money into wealth centres in pricey premises
description: HSBC Holdings and its subsidiary Hang Seng Bank, Standard Chartered Bank and China Citic Bank International (CNCBI) are among the major lenders rushing to open luxury wealth centres in Hong Kong to capture high-net-worth customers. HSBC last week opened its fifth such centre, on the 58th floor of Two International Finance Centre, with 13,000 sq ft and 34 meeting rooms with harbour views. This is in addition to its similar centres located in the HSBC building in Central, the International...

title: Oil Prices Plunge Below $100 on Iran Deal Optimism
description: Oil prices plunged in early Asian trade on Monday following reports over the weekend that a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz was in its final stages. At the time of writing, both oil benchmarks had fallen by more than 5%, with Brent breaking back below $100 to trade at $98.27, while WTI fell to $91.63. Optimism around a deal was tempered somewhat on Sunday when President Trump posted on social media that he had informed his representatives "not to rush into a deal". A senior U.S. administration official then added that, while progress had been…
